behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive directory events), not a single function. MOCK-MISS: bug appears at watcher↔OS event boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fake events can miss platform-specific recursion gaps. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert one transformed output is produced without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets dedup logic itself (same path + time window), which is local state/algorithm. MOCK-MISS: no required module boundary in wording. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required for this specific claim. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks are negative (no real infra, no AI, no multi-step agent chain). Rejected higher levels: behavior does not mention watcher events or OS timing races, only dedup rule semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component directly with controlled timestamps/config: first request accepted, second same-path request within window rejected, request after window accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events. REAL-INFRA is required; mock event streams can hide watcher backend quirks. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic fakes often miss real debounce/coalescing behavior. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real watched directory, perform a single write pattern known to trigger multiple fs events on the platform (e.g., write+rename/flush), then assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level directory lifecycle behavior (delete/recreate inode/path semantics) while process remains live. MOCK-MISS: failure appears in watcher re-subscription after real filesystem mutation. MOCK-HIDE: stubs usually preserve logical path identity and can miss broken reattach behavior. Rejected higher levels: no model decision quality or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watched directory, remove it, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without daemon restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on interaction of concurrent filesystem arrivals, timestamp retrieval, and scheduler/queue handoff. Although sorting can be unit-tested, this behavior’s real failure mode is often at watcher+metadata boundary under real arrival timing. REAL-INFRA likely needed to reproduce timestamp granularity/ties and event ordering quirks. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependence and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real watched directory, create multiple files in a tight burst with controlled creation times (or staggered writes), then assert processing order matches observed file creation timestamps.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem timing/partial-write boundary. MOCK-MISS: issue appears when event timing, file locks/flush semantics, and reader behavior interact. MOCK-HIDE: fake file APIs usually do atomic writes and miss partial-content races. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real directory, start a slow/streaming write to a file to trigger arrival before completion, verify daemon delays processing until write is finished and output reflects complete content.
